column9: Richèl's sessions had a good pace, the rest were way too fast with too little time for exercises and too little time for breaks.
column12: Use Richèl's teaching style for all topics. Keep the amount of content in the course as it is, but spread it over 5 days instead of 4 days. There's simply way too much to go through, way to many potential technical difficulties that take time, and way too little time to do exercises to actually absorb all the content and enjoy the learning process. And I would suggest that the course on how to connect and work in an HPC environment be moved to before this course during the same semester, so that people don't have to deal with having to learn that at the same time. Right now the "Online training events for new users: NAISS Introduction training days" course is after this course during the semester, which makes little sense considering that its contents should be a prerequisite for this course.

column12: Today (day3), the topics were more of what I expected from an Introduction to Python and Using Python in an HPC environment course. The morning session about pandas, matplotlib and seaborn was interesting, as well as the big data with python. I will probably use Dask in the future so it was a good explanation of how Dask works. I still don't understand the time spent talking about bash and sending jobs. We could use that time learning and using common python packages in an HPC cluster.
